channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tom Trbojevic suffers another hamstring injury in Manly’s big win over Cowboys
item.description: Manly Sea Eagles will be concerned over another hamstring injury to their star fullback and captain, Tom Trbojevic, after a commanding 38-6 victory against the North Queensland Cowboys in Townsville. ...

item.title: Sid Lowe discusses Spanish football stories and answers readers’ questions
item.description: In a live Q&A hosted by The Guardian, Sid Lowe, the newspaper’s veteran Spanish football correspondent based in Madrid, responded to readers’ questions about various aspects of Spanish football and th...

item.title: CBSE Class 12 Result 2026 Expected by April End, Students Advised to Check Official Portals for Updates
item.description: According to recent reports, the Central Board of Secondary Education (CBSE) is expected to declare the Class 12 examination results for 2026 by the end of April. More than 18 lakh students across Ind...

item.title: Africa Health Worker Shortage and Colonial Legacy: Why Skilled Professionals Dey Migrate
item.description: My people, this article dey talk about how Africa dey seriously lose her doctors, nurses and other health workers just when we need them most. According to WHO, Africa go need between 5 to 6 million m...

item.title: Father loses six children after cult-linked prison contact and deadly fast
item.description: In January 2025, a call from Shimo La Tewa Prison pulled a father back into a deadly cult, leading to the deaths of his six children. The father, a survivor of the Shakahola tragedy in 2023, was conta...

item.title: Kaizer Chiefs target top-three finish with Polokwane City clash
item.description: Kaizer Chiefs are aiming to secure a top-three finish in the league with an important match against Polokwane City at the Old Peter Mokaba Stadium this Saturday. Co-coach Khalil Ben Youssef stressed t...

item.title: IMF Forecasts Slight Increase In Nigeria’s Debt-To-GDP Ratio By 2027
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) don project say Nigeria debt-to-GDP ratio go climb to 33.1 percent for 2027 from 32.3 percent wey dem expect for 2026. This projection follow after President Bola...

item.title: Pakistan Mediates Between the U.S. and Iran Amid Ongoing Regional Tensions
item.description: Pakistan's army chief visited Tehran on Thursday to engage with Iranian officials in an effort to extend the fragile ceasefire that paused nearly seven weeks of hostilities involving Iran, the U.S., a...

item.title: Democrats Criticize FIFA Over High Transportation Costs and Ticket Prices for 2026 World Cup in New York and New Jersey
item.description: Democrats in New York and New Jersey are targeting FIFA as a new symbol of affordability issues ahead of the 2026 World Cup. The soccer governing body is being blamed for high costs passed on to local...

item.title: Delhi University student raises concern over dress code issue at Nari Shakti event
item.description: A Delhi University student, Saarah Sharma of Daulat Ram College, has alleged that she was stopped from going on stage to felicitate a union minister at the 'Nari Shakti: Viksit Bharat ki Awaaz' event ...

item.title: Table Mountain Nominated Again for Africa’s Leading Tourist Attraction
item.description: Table Mountain in Cape Town has once again been nominated for Africa’s Leading Tourist Attraction at the prestigious World Travel Awards. This marks the mountain’s sixth consideration for the title, h...

item.title: Massive Attack returns with Boots on the Ground featuring Tom Waits
item.description: After a decade-long hiatus from releasing purchasable music, Massive Attack has returned with their new single 'Boots on the Ground', featuring Tom Waits. The track is emblematic of the band's evoluti...

item.title: Diplomatic Efforts Intensify as US and Iran Approach Potential Agreement Amid Regional Tensions
item.description: Diplomatic initiatives are advancing as the United States and Iran appear closer to a potential deal, with a second round of talks likely to occur in Pakistan. Key issues under discussion include Iran...

item.title: Hugo Ekitike sidelined with Achilles injury, to miss World Cup and rest of season
item.description: Liverpool forward Hugo Ekitike don suffer serious setback as he rupture him Achilles tendon for UEFA Champions League quarter-final second-leg match against Paris Saint-Germain for Anfield. The 23-yea...

item.title: Meghan discusses experiencing unprecedented online harassment
item.description: Meghan, the Duchess of Sussex, has openly spoken about the extent of online trolling she has endured, describing herself as the most trolled person in the world. The statement highlights the extreme l...

item.title: Haziribag Man Dies in Lake Fishing Accident
item.description: A tragic incident occurred in Haziribag, Jharkhand, where a man lost his life while fishing in a local lake. Details surrounding the circumstances of the drowning are still emerging, but authorities h...

item.title: Court of Appeal overturns Benjamin Field’s murder conviction of university lecturer Peter Farquhar
item.description: Benjamin Field, a former churchwarden and son of a Baptist minister, has had his 2019 murder conviction quashed by the Court of Appeal. Field had been sentenced to life imprisonment for the killing of...

item.title: Kim Ji Soo Joins Marithé + François Girbaud Philippines as Brand Ambassador
item.description: Filipino fans of Korean entertainment have another reason to celebrate as renowned Korean actor Kim Ji Soo has officially become the latest brand ambassador for Marithé + François Girbaud Philippines....

item.title: MastiFest 2026 Gala Dinner celebrates elegance and cultural flair in Umhlanga
item.description: Masti Productions hosted an evening filled with glamour, sophistication and cultural celebration at the MastiFest 2026 Gala Dinner, held at the Premier Hotel Umhlanga. The black-tie event drew a varie...

item.title: Strengthening Democracy through Comprehensive Voter Education in South Africa
item.description: The article argues that South Africa urgently needs reforms to make voting easier, including automatic voter eligibility at age 18. It stresses the importance of extensive voter education that goes be...
